input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register routes not in the visual hierarchy
        Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (e.g. from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

**Receiving the parameter** in `ProductDetailsPage`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    private string _productId;
    public string ProductId
    {
        get => _productId;
        set { _productId = value; LoadProduct(value); }
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- `FlyoutItem` creates top-level flyout entries. Wrapping two `ShellContent` items inside a `Tab` gives you the Active/Archived tab bar under Products.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until navigated to.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is needed for pages **not** in the visual tree (like detail pages). The route is relative, so from the active-products page Shell resolves it as a push onto the navigation stack.

1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

This is a straightforward code generation request - no need to explore a codebase. I'll provide the XAML and the code-behind.
